tweets.text:
Trump made 3,642 trades in Q1 2026.

That annualizes out to be 14,568 trades a year.

Or just about 58 trades a day.

Clearly someone in his camp is daytrading on his behalf but guhhh damn, 58 trades a day?! Lol

For comparison sakes, Nancy Pelosi made roughly 12 trades in 2025.

tweets.text: Trading 101 right there
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Magic Formula: Few understand and fewer implement.

Calculation:
%Account invested = 100 X (%Account risk/%position risk)

Example: If, per trade, you are risking 0.5% of your account.
If the position risk on a given ticker is less than 1%

In a single trade, you will be invested
tweets.full_text: Trading 101 right there

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

Visible AIS traffic is observed mainly in the Iran route. The Oman route has gone completely dark for inbound tankers.

Based on inbound tankers, they all used Iran route. I will need to confirm later using satellite data how many tankers went in through the Oman route.

The video is from @MarineTraffic and I have included my table for inbound tankers.
tweets.full_text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

Visible AIS traffic is observed mainly in the Iran route. The Oman route has gone completely dark for inbound tankers.

Based on inbound tankers, they all used Iran route. I will need to confirm later using satellite data how many tankers went in through the Oman route.

The video is from @MarineTraffic and I have included my table for inbound tankers.

tweets.text:
EIA’s petroleum supply monthly report for April showed ending US commercial crude storage at 451.997 million bbls versus EIA’s 457.812 million bbls. 

This means that the incoming weekly inventory reports will have to adjust inventory down by 5.185 million bbls.

tweets.text:
Honestly surprised we don't see more L1s move to natively sponsor gas for stablecoin tx's.

Just makes sense as far as onboarding non-crypto native users.

Also makes sense for more blockchains from a revenue perspective - e.g. same type of idea as the Open USD announcement... less dependency on the duopoly of Circle / Tether and utilizing the yield to reinvest in the ecosystem (e.g. sponsored txs) rather than leaking it out to another stablecoin issuer
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Is it really an agent if it stops mid-task?

Gasless transfers on Sui keep agentic workflows running: agents keep calling APIs, executing tasks, and operating autonomously.

No freezes. No downtime. https://t.co/Iw3WklXzvS
tweets.full_text:
Honestly surprised we don't see more L1s move to natively sponsor gas for stablecoin tx's.

Just makes sense as far as onboarding non-crypto native users.

Also makes sense for more blockchains from a revenue perspective - e.g. same type of idea as the Open USD announcement... less dependency on the duopoly of Circle / Tether and utilizing the yield to reinvest in the ecosystem (e.g. sponsored txs) rather than leaking it out to another stablecoin issuer
